     The Numbers Business Report

Second Act (2018)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $39,282,227Details
International Box Office $33,011,839
Worldwide Box Office $72,294,066
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,203,309
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $849,267
Total $2,052,576
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A 40-year-old woman is struggling with frustrations of not having achieved more in life. As an experienced, quick-witted, perceptive employee, she is passed over for a promotion solely because she doesn’t have a college degree. Until, that is, she gets the chance to prove that street smarts are as valuable as book smarts, and that it is never too late for a second act.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,489,983 (16.5% of total gross)
Legs:5.34 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:54.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$15,700,000 (worldwide box office is 4.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,607 opening theaters/2,607 max. theaters, 4.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $51,458,617

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 21st, 2018 (Wide) by STX Entertainment
International Releases: December 7th, 2018 (Wide) (Australia)
December 14th, 2018 (Wide), released as Втори шанс (Bulgaria)
December 14th, 2018 (Wide), released as Antrasis šansas (Lithuania)
December 21st, 2018 (Wide), released as Hayatım Yalan (Turkey)
December 21st, 2018 (Wide) (Netherlands)
Video Release: March 12th, 2019 by Universal Home Entertainment
June 3rd, 2019 by Sony Pictures
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some crude sexual references, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2530 (Cert #51638), 6/6/2018)
Running Time: 104 minutes
Plot point: False Identity, Mid-Life Crisis, Promotion, Unexpected Families
Social setting: Corporate Life
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: STX Entertainment, Huayi Brothers Pictures Ltd, Nuyorican
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A 40-year-old woman is struggling with frustrations of not having achieved more in life. As an experienced, quick-witted, perceptive employee, she is passed over for a promotion solely because she doesn’t have a college degree. Until, that is, she gets the chance to prove that street smarts are as valuable as book smarts, and that it is never too late for a second act.
